tier_justification: Tier 1 predicate: 2 work-units (sanitiser in bridge/api.ts wired into sendChunked; plain-text rule in prompts/system.md), above tier 0 because no existing test or verify-criterion covers the goal state, and below tier 2 because <3 work-units and the change itself has no irreversible/outward surface (the post-merge launchd restart is reversible ops, not part of the diff).

evals.assert: A markdown-laden reply pushed through the real exported sendChunked (stub transport, fresh clone of the PR branch) reaches Telegram's sendMessage body with no markdown tokens (**, *, __, ##, backticks, ](...)) while all human-readable content and the link URL survive.
evals.evidence: Fresh clone at 9c1381cbe84deda36a412601226671a4870a0333: probe printed 'E1 PASS' (no markdown tokens leaked, all content and the URL survived). Negative control on base d5c21ed: probe failed with AssertionError 'markdown tokens leaked' and the harness printed 'negative control ok: probe fails on base SHA (no sanitiser)'. Graded 2026-07-08.

evals.assert: prompts/system.md at the PR head contains a behavioural rule instructing that replies are read in Telegram (and the terminal REPL) and must be written as plain conversational text — no markdown headers/bold/tables/code fences unless quoting actual code — phrased consistently with the file's existing rule style.
evals.verifier_instruction: Fresh clone: git clone -q --depth 1 -b fix/telegram-reply-formatting https://github.com/blueman82/assistant-agent.git into a temp dir. Read prompts/system.md IN FULL. PASS only if (1) a rule exists stating replies are read in Telegram/terminal and must be plain conversational text without markdown formatting (headers, bold, tables, code fences), (2) it allows an exception for quoting actual code, (3) it sits in a sensible section (e.g. Ground rules) and matches the file's existing bullet style. Return pass/fail per criterion with the rule quoted verbatim as evidence. You receive only this instruction and the clone — no implementation conversation.
evals.evidence: Independent verifier agent (fresh clone, no implementation context) returned PASS on all 3 criteria, quoting the rule verbatim from prompts/system.md line 74 in Ground rules: '**Plain text replies** — your replies are read in Telegram and the terminal REPL, not a markdown renderer. Write plain conversational text: no headers, no bold/italic markers, no tables, no code fences unless quoting actual code. Simple hyphen bullets are fine'. Verifier's clone was at d6999cd; prompts/system.md is byte-identical at head 9c1381c (git diff d6999cd..9c1381c -- prompts/system.md is empty), so the verdict transfers. Graded 2026-07-08.

evals.assert: Fresh clone of the PR branch typechecks and passes the full test suite (typecheck and test run as separate commands per the documented hang quirk).
evals.cmd:
set -e
d=$(mktemp -d)
git clone -q --depth 1 -b fix/telegram-reply-formatting https://github.com/blueman82/assistant-agent.git "$d"
cd "$d"
npm install --silent >/dev/null 2>&1
npm run typecheck
npm test
evals.negative_control:
set -e
d=$(mktemp -d)
git clone -q --depth 1 -b fix/telegram-reply-formatting https://github.com/blueman82/assistant-agent.git "$d"
cd "$d"
npm install --silent >/dev/null 2>&1
printf 'import { test } from "node:test";\nimport assert from "node:assert/strict";\ntest("neg control", () => { assert.equal(1, 2); });\n' > bridge/__neg.test.ts
if npm test >/dev/null 2>&1; then echo 'NEG CONTROL FAILED: suite passed with injected failing test'; exit 1; else echo 'negative control ok: suite fails when a failing test is injected'; fi
evals.evidence: Fresh clone at 9c1381c: npm install, npm run typecheck (clean), npm test ran as separate commands — suite ended PASS (85 tests incl. probe_conventions.test.sh 'PASS'). Negative control: injected bridge/__neg.test.ts asserting 1==2 made npm test fail; harness printed 'E3 negative control ok: suite fails when a failing test is injected'. Graded 2026-07-08.

evals.assert: The PR introduces no parse_mode anywhere in the bridge send path (parse_mode HTML/MarkdownV2 was explicitly rejected: a malformed entity loses the reply entirely).
evals.cmd: set -e; d=$(mktemp -d); git clone -q --depth 1 -b fix/telegram-reply-formatting https://github.com/blueman82/assistant-agent.git "$d"; cd "$d"; if grep -n parse_mode bridge/api.ts bridge/telegram-bridge.ts; then echo 'FAIL: parse_mode introduced against frozen decision'; exit 1; else echo 'PASS: no parse_mode in bridge send path'; fi
evals.negative_control: printf 'body.parse_mode = "HTML"' | grep -q parse_mode && echo 'negative control ok: grep detects parse_mode when present'
evals.evidence: First run at d6999cd FAILED: grep matched the literal token inside two doc comments in bridge/api.ts (no functional use). Fixed by rewording the comments (9c1381c) rather than weakening the frozen grep. Re-run in fresh clone at 9c1381c: grep empty, printed 'E4 PASS: no parse_mode in bridge send path'; negative control detected an injected occurrence. Graded 2026-07-08.

evals.assert: Already-plain text passes through sendChunked byte-identical: list hyphens, a genuine asterisk in maths, and a URL containing an underscore are not mangled by the sanitiser.
evals.evidence: Fresh clone at 9c1381c: probe printed 'E5 PASS' — hyphen bullets, spaced maths, underscore URL, plain sentence all byte-identical through sendChunked. Negative control with E5_CORRUPT=1 (input **bold**) failed the equality check as required. Graded 2026-07-08.
